issues.evidence: The draft keeps the fake Lu Chenzhou command as an observed external voice event and does not reveal any formal RV system name to outsiders.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the mimicry as an observed command imitation only; do not upgrade it into full monster ecology or public golden-finger exposure.

issues.evidence: Lu Chenzhou keeps the RV closed, uses lights / horn / reverse / angle rather than opening the door, and preserves the retreat line throughout the chapter.
issues.suggested_fix: Preserve this closed-vehicle boundary in revision and later state updates.

issues.evidence: The abdominal bleeding man and low-fever child remain outside and are kept in the light / no-sound rule set, while Zhou Xiaohe remains temporary observation only.
issues.suggested_fix: Do not record boarding, ally promotion, or crew status for any of these characters.

issues.evidence: The chapter stays at the Qinghe medical-point exterior and does not enter the main building or explain the survivor organization.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the scope exterior-only; the next chapter can handle the trade without a full internal reveal.

issues.evidence: The external material bags remain at risk, the rear seal stays under pressure, and the filter window keeps shrinking while the chapter ends on a negotiated medical trade.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the material bags external and treat the rear seal stress as ongoing rather than resolved.

proposed_state_updates: Chapter 15 at Qinghe medical-point exterior: the fake Lu Chenzhou command '门开了，上车' continues to draw survivors and fog dogs, but Lu Chenzhou reasserts command authority through car lights, vehicle angle, horn, and closed-door rules.

Lin Zhixia establishes and enforces an exterior triage line: controllable survivors follow light, pollution-suspect bodies stay away from the door radius, and any attempt to push a contaminated casualty toward the RV is treated as risk transfer rather than rescue.
The abdominal bleeding man and low-fever child remain outside but controllable; they survive by obeying light / no-sound instructions and do not board.
Fog dogs are temporarily displaced by light, sound, and metal reflection, but are not cleared; they remain an external pressure against the vehicle and the hanging material bags.
The external isolation bags remain attached to the rear rack and are still at risk from fog-dog attacks or survivor pressure.
The medical-point interior reveals a stable surgical lamp and a credible human broadcaster who offers a trade: save one key injured person in exchange for No. 2 power-box permission.
The Simple Medical Bay remains uninstalled and inactive; no new module unlock occurs in chapter 15.
human_approval_needed: Approve the fake Lu Chenzhou command as observed voice mimicry without revealing the RV system to outsiders.
Approve the exterior triage line and risk-transfer refusal as the chapter's core survival rule.
Approve the fog dogs as temporarily displaced rather than defeated.
Approve the chapter ending on a real human trade offer for No. 2 power-box permission.
Approve that the Simple Medical Bay stays uninstalled and inactive.
